arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Joao Felix 'finalises' deadline day switch to Serie A
arts.sum: Joao Felix reportedly signs the necessary paperwork to join AC Milan on loan from Chelsea until the end of the season.

arts.ttl: Graham Potter denied fairytale Chelsea return but West Ham offer genuine signs of encouragement
arts.sum: Hammers show fight despite lack of cutting edge

arts.ttl: Chelsea may have Joao Felix transfer regret as "very upset" attacker suffers injury in West Ham win
arts.sum: Chelsea suffer a fresh injury scare in their 2-1 victory over London rivals West Ham United, as Marc Guiu sustains a problem right at the death.

arts.ttl: Chelsea fans were mightily impressed with Enzo Maresca in victory over West Ham, ‘actually working’…
arts.sum: He deserves credit for their win!

arts.ttl: Chelsea v West Ham player ratings
arts.sum: Chelsea came from behind to beat West Ham and move up to fourth in the Premier League table. Here’s how we rated each Blues player in the 2-1 win at Stamford Bridge. Filip Jorgensen: 6 Selected in place of the dropped Robert Sanchez, the Dane could do nothing to prevent Jarrod Bowen from netting for […] The post Chelsea v West Ham player ratings appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: West Ham player ratings vs Chelsea: Jarrod Bowen brings a spark as team-mates struggle
arts.sum: The forward marked his return from injury with a goal in the defeat

arts.ttl: Gary Neville praises 'threatening' player in Chelsea's 2-1 win vs West Ham, he 'injected something' into the game
Chelsea back into top four after beating West Ham and former boss Graham Potter
arts.sum: Aaron Wan-Bissaka’s own-goal in the 74th minute, from Cole Palmer’s effort, was enough to seal victory for the Blues at Stamford Bridge.

arts.ttl: Manchester United transfer update: Alejandro Garnacho drops major hint on future in statement amidst Chelsea links
arts.sum: Manchester United forward Alejandro Garnacho sends out a message to fans amidst speculation that he could leave the club in the near future.

arts.ttl: Arsenal and Chelsea's late transfer deadline day Victor Osimhen hope explained
arts.sum: However, the Turkish transfer window still has another week left to go and, as such, Galatasaray have plenty of time to complete a deal to replace Osimhen if they were to lose him. Unfortunately, with little time remaining before the 11pm deadline, Arsenal and Chelsea would struggle to get that deal done and as such, they may have to wait until the summer to revisit a move for the Nigerian striker.
